name: ACLU Foundation
mission: The ACLU seeks to defend and preserve democracy and the individual rights and liberties that the Constitution and laws of the United States guarantee everyone in this country.
programs: Trone Center for Justice and Equality
theoryOfChange: The ACLU Foundation has a published 'TVTP Grant Program Logic Model' on its website (aclu.org). This document, released on December 15, 2022, is described as a 'Policy Document' that explains the goals and objectives of the TVTP program and how they can be achieved.

scoreSummary: American Civil Liberties Union Foundation, Inc. shows strong impact and moderate alignment.

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

components.improvement_suggestion: Add a dedicated zakat page or clearly state whether donations are zakat-eligible.
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

alignment.rationale: Alignment 26/50: MEDIUM Muslim donor fit, advocacy cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; good program ratio (≥75%) suggests reasonable delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: Publish a time-bound plan to deploy excess unrestricted reserves into mission delivery, while keeping a clear operating reserve floor.
components.name: Financial Health

score_details.score_summary: American Civil Liberties Union Foundation, Inc. shows strong impact and moderate alignment.

zakat.claim_evidence: Organization is in zakat denylist (partners but doesn't collect directly) (Source: https://www.aclu.org/sites/default/files/pdfs/humanrights/blockingfaith_execsumm.pdf)

all_citations.claim: The organization reports a program expense ratio of 82.5% and holds 40.5 months of working capital.
Total revenue for the period was $185,146,988.
Donation options are available for supporters to contribute to the organization's mission through general charitable giving.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: The foundation earns a 62/100 score due to its high financial efficiency and substantial working capital, though its focus on broad advocacy rather than direct aid results in a moderate impact score.
areas_for_improvement: Limited direct service metrics as the organization focuses primarily on high-level capacity building and advocacy
Moderate alignment for donors seeking specific religious or community-focused outcomes

dimension_explanations.alignment: The organization offers a medium fit for donors, focusing on broad constitutional advocacy and systemic change rather than direct community services [1]. While its cause urgency is high due to its role in national legal defense, it remains a general sadaqah-eligible option for those prioritizing civil rights [3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The foundation demonstrates high effectiveness in capacity building, allocating 82.5% of its $185,146,988 revenue directly to programs [1][2]. Its financial health is further evidenced by a significant reserve of 40.5 months of working capital, ensuring long-term stability for its legal initiatives [1].

baseline_narrative.headline: The American Civil Liberties Union Foundation, Inc. serves as a premier legal advocate dedicated to preserving the constitutional rights and individual liberties of all people in the United States.
strengths: High program expense ratio of 82.5% ensuring most funds reach core advocacy work
Exceptional fundraising efficiency at only $0.10 per $1 raised
Strong liquidity with 40.5 months of working capital available
baseline_narrative.summary: The organization operates critical initiatives including the Center for Liberty, the Southern Collective, and the State Supreme Court Initiative to defend democratic principles [1]. With a total revenue of $185,146,988, the foundation maintains a robust financial position to support its nationwide advocacy and litigation efforts [2]. Supporters can contribute to these efforts through various sadaqah-eligible giving methods to sustain long-term legal protections [3].
